Preferred_Parent.Parent_String: synthetic organic blue pigment [300013303], blue pigment [300013182], pigment by color [300013134], pigments [300013109], colorant (material) [300013026], materials by function [300014692], materials (substances) [300010358], Materials (hierarchy name) [300010357], Materials Facet [300264091]

Descriptive_Note.Note_Text: Any of several deep blue pigments consisting chiefly of ferric ferrocyanide; first developed in Berlin in 1704 by Diesbach. It is made by adding ferric chloride to a boiling solution of hexacyano ferrate. Prussian blue has deep blue, finely divided particles that are transparent in watercolors; it has high tinting strength and is stable to light, although it turns brown in the presence of alkalis or heat. It is used as a colorant in cyanotypes, blueprint paper, laundry blue, linoleum, leather, plastics, paper, cosmetics, and formerly as a textile dye for silk, cotton, and wool, where it was mordanted with ferric oxide.

Source.Source_ID: 2000047822/MFA Conservation and Art Material Encyclopedia Online: CAMEO (2005-)

Descriptive_Note.Note_Text: 指多個主要由亞鐵氰化鐵構成的深藍色顏料中任何一種，由狄斯巴赫（Diesbach）在西元1704年首度發明於柏林，在滾沸的鐵氰化溶液中加進氯化鐵而成。普魯士藍具有經過精細分割的深藍粒子，在水彩中呈透明。其著色度高，對光呈穩定反應，不過遇到鹼性物質或加熱時就會變成棕色。普魯士藍可做為鋅板攝影、藍圖紙、洗衣藍、油氈、皮革、塑膠、紙張、化妝品的著色劑；以前曾以氧化鐵媒染普魯士藍，做為絲綢、棉花、羊毛的紡織染料。

Source.Source_ID: 2000026701/Mayer, Dictionary of Art Terms and Techniques (1969)
